pi_items.source: Task description (line 5) and standard Bridge Command installation structure
Task description: 'ending with a sudden head-on collision risk exactly at T+4.0 hours (240 minutes)'
Verifier.py line 149: 'tolerance = 0.1 # +/- 6 minutes'. Verified: 0.1 hours = 6 minutes

Kinematic calculation: Own ship (12 kts, 4h) moves 0.8°N to 49.8°. Target (18 kts, 4h) must move 1.2°S from starting position. Starting position: 49.8 + 1.2 = 51.0°. Verified via verifier.py formula: distance=120nm, rel_speed=30kts, time=4.0h
Calculated correspondence to time_tolerance: ±0.05° latitude → ±0.1 hour time tolerance. At 50.95°: time=3.9h. At 51.05°: time=4.1h. Tolerance range matches time_tolerance_hours exactly.

privileged_info_summary: Bridge Command is a real, free, open-source ship simulator (GitHub: bridgecommand/bc, GPL v2 licensed). The task creates a synthetic human factors experiment scenario named 'Exp) Vigilance Study 4H' in /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/. The scenario requires an own ship (MV Subject) at latitude 49.0° traveling north at 12 knots to intercept a target vessel (MV Stimulus) starting at latitude 51.0° traveling south at 18 knots, with collision occurring exactly 4.0 hours (±0.1 hour tolerance) after start. All metadata parameters are mathematically consistent: expected target latitude of 51.0° is derived from head-on closure kinematics over 4 hours, and latitude tolerance of ±0.05° precisely corresponds to the time tolerance of ±0.1 hours.
